items.title: Police find 40-year-old woman’s body in Seremban flat, believed dead for nearly a week; no foul play suspected
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 13 — A 40-year-old local woman was found dead in an apartment in Ampangan here Saturday. Seremban district police chief ACP Mohd Yatim Osman said police received a report at 5.39 pm after receiving information from members of the public about an unpleasant smell believed to be coming from the residential unit. He said a team of police officers and personnel, together with the Seremban Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM), went to the scene to conduct an inspection.

items.title: Pas 'not disappointed' despite Johor whitewash, says Hadi
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Pas is not disappointed over its failure to win any seats in the Johor state election as the state remains under Malay-Muslim leadership, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said. He said Pas was willing to make sacrifices by directing its members to support Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in areas where the party did not contest to ensure Malay-Muslim political power was maintained. "Pas was willing to sacrifice by directing members to support BN candidates in areas we did not contest, including MCA candidates," he said at the Pas Negeri Sembilan Perdana Dinner in Paroi last night.

items.title: Malaysia rolls out royal welcome for Singapore President Tharman in his first state visit
items.summary: SEPANG, July 13 — Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam arrived in Malaysia last night for a three-day state visit from July 13 to 15 at the invitation of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. This is Tharman’s first state visit to Malaysia since assuming office as the ninth President of Singapore on Sept 14, 2023. Total bilateral trade between Malaysia and Singapore reached RM402.35 billion (US$93.97 billion) in 2025, representing an increase of 1.5 per cent compared with 2024.

items.title: Wisma Putra confirms death of Malaysian scaling Europe’s highest peak in Russia
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 13 — A Malaysian died while climbing Mount Elbrus in Russia on Saturday (July 11), the Foreign Ministry confirmed. In a statement on Sunday, the ministry, also known as Wisma Putra, said the deceased was among three Malaysians taking part in a Mount Elbrus climbing expedition organised by a local operator. The ministry also extended its deepest condolences to the family of the deceased.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: MPs to grill govt on UN reform, food crisis plans and military readiness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Malaysia’s stance on the imbalance created by the veto power among the world’s superpowers in the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) and the government’s preparedness to address a projected food supply crisis are among the key issues to be raised in the Dewan Rakyat today. Shaharizukirnain Abd Kadir (PN–Setiu) will ask the Agriculture and Food Security Minister to state the government’s comprehensive contingency plan to address the projected food supply crisis, as well as the effectiveness of incentives channelled to food-producing states in boosting domestic self-sufficiency amid rising agricultural input costs linked to the West Asia crisis. The sitting will also see seven government Bills being tabled for first reading, including the Communications and Multimedia (Amendment) Bill 2026, Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (Amendment) Bill 2026, and Social Work Profession Bill 2026.

items.title: Hadi says Pas-Umno seat negotiations ongoing for Negri polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Pas is prepared to accept an Umno leader as the Negri Sembilan menteri besar candidate if cooperation between the two parties is finalised for the Aug 1 state election. Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said seat negotiations between both sides were ongoing. Hadi said the discussions included Umno retaining its 14 seats, while Pas would keep its three existing seats.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi batters eastern China, threatens days of heavy rain
items.summary: WENZHOU: Typhoon Bavi, the most powerful storm to strike mainland China this year, brought heavy rain to the eastern coast on Sunday and lashed densely populated cities with violent winds, testing the country's ability to cope with extreme weather. Bavi had weakened by Sunday morning to a tropical storm as it pushed inland, but forecasters warned that the France-sized storm system could unleash prolonged and widespread rain across eastern and northern China in the coming days. Bavi struck Zhejiang's coastal city of Yuhuan at around 11.20pm (1520 GMT) on Saturday before making a second landfall in Yueqing, part of the city of Wenzhou, at around midnight.

items.title: Oil jumps three pct after US, Iran escalate strikes in Mideast
items.summary: Oil prices jumped on Monday as Iran expanded strikes on Gulf states following attacks by the United States, threatening energy shipments via the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude futures climbed US$2.34, or 3.08 per cent, to US$78.35 by 2311 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate crude rose US$2.21, or 3.09 per cent, to US$73.62 a barrel. US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial traffic, although Iran declared earlier that it closed the strait after a vessel traveled on an unapproved route and was struck.

items.title: 'Pray for us,' says Bella ahead of Syed Saddiq appeal ruling [WATCH]
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Actress and singer Bella Astillah said she was filled with emotion ahead of the Federal Court's decision on the prosecution's appeal against Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman's acquittal on four corruption-related charges. Bella was among the first to arrive at the Palace of Justice, joining Syed Saddiq's family as they awaited the court's decision. Syed Saddiq has yet to arrive.

items.title: Tributes paid to US senator and Trump ally Lindsey Graham
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a South Carolina Republican and key Trump ally who was elected to the Senate four times, died aged 71 following a "brief and sudden illness," his office said Sunday. "Senator Lindsey Graham, one of the greatest people and Senators I have ever known, is dead! - FORMER PRESIDENT JOE BIDEN - Former president Joe Biden said he and his wife were shocked by Graham's sudden death.

items.title: Vietnam arrests speedboat captain after deadly capsize
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnamese police arrested on Sunday the captain of a speedboat that capsized near southern Phu Quoc island, killing 15 Indian tourists, authorities said. The incident occurred on Saturday as the boat carrying 32 Indian tourists, three Vietnamese crew members and a tour guide overturned in rough seas off May Rut Ngoai islet, state media said. Several people were drifting at sea and not wearing life jackets, Loc said, according to state outlet VNExpress.

items.title: Recurring income anchors Tropicana's growth strategy
items.summary: Group chief executive officer Dennis Ng said the company is strengthening its recurring income portfolio through an asset-light business model, broadening its presence beyond township development into hospitality and other complementary businesses. "The partnership reflects Tropicana's broader strategy to build sustainable recurring income streams that complement its core township development business. Ng said strategic partnerships allow Tropicana to extract greater value from its developments by enhancing project positioning, strengthening buyer confidence and creating professionally managed hospitality assets capable of generating long-term recurring income.

items.title: Tropicana drives growth through Accor tie-up
items.summary: The property developer's partnership with global hospitality group Accor SA to launch Malaysia's first branded Accor condotel, Mercure Living Genting Highlands, represents a key milestone in this strategy. Through an asset-light partnership model, the collaboration enables Tropicana to unlock greater value from its landbank while integrating international hospitality expertise into its developments and creating sustainable recurring income streams. The collaboration, through a hotel management agreement, will integrate Accor's hospitality expertise into selected Tropicana developments, starting with Tropicana Grandhill, a 45.33ha wellness-focused precinct within the 241.19ha Tropicana WindCity integrated township in Genting Highlands.

items.title: SD Property's UniRazak project seen with upside from higher plot ratio
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd's proposed RM900 million redevelopment of the Universiti Tun Abdul Razak (UniRazak) site in Kuala Lumpur could deliver further upside if it secures a higher development intensity under the Kuala Lumpur Local Plan 2040. HLIB views the acquisition positively, as the implied land cost of RM252 per square foot per plot ratio (psf ppr) and land cost-to-GDV ratio of 17.8 per cent are slightly below recent nearby transactions by Mah Sing Group Bhd and Paramount Corp Bhd. Mah Sing acquired the Corus Hotel site in August 2025 at RM287 psf ppr, while Paramount purchased a Jalan U-Thant site at RM267 psf ppr in March 2026.

items.title: Tariff hike to lift Westports earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Higher container tariffs are expected to more than offset rising fuel costs at Westports Holdings Bhd, supporting stronger earnings in the second quarter of financial year 2026 (Q2 FY26), according to RHB Research. RHB Research expects core profit after tax and minority interests (Patami) to come in between RM280 million and RM320 million, representing year-on-year growth of about 20 per cent to 40 per cent. The firm said the stronger earnings outlook would be supported by higher container handling charges and continued growth in container throughput.

items.title: Four headwinds threaten Malaysia's market recovery in 2H
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy is expected to remain on a solid footing in the second half of 2026, underpinned by resilient domestic demand, robust electrical and electronics (E&E) exports and a stable inflation outlook. The research house said the main risks include lingering global supply chain disruptions, a stronger US dollar, rising political uncertainty ahead of a possible early general election and the proposed expansion of the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI. HLIB said this could temporarily weaken the ringgit and dampen sentiment in the domestic equity market.

items.title: Why is GDP not enough?
items.summary: Part 1 - Beyond GDP: Understanding Sabah and Sarawak Through Regional Input-Output Analysis GDP Explains Growth, Not Economic KUALA LUMPUR: Structure Malaysia has long relied on standard economic indicators such as Gross Domestic Product (GDP), GDP per capita, investment inflows, and public expenditure to measure regional development. This limitation is especially relevant for Sabah and Sarawak. Sarawak contributed around RM148.2 billion and Sabah RM84.3 billion, underscoring East Malaysia's strategic importance to national output.

items.title: Can Johor start a blue wave?
items.summary: BARISAN Nasional's victory in the Johor election should be viewed as more than just an electoral success. The result may signal a political shift that could affect future elections. Johor has historically occupied a unique position in Malaysia's political landscape.

items.title: Johor election: PH miscalculated, say analysts [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan's failure to tailor its campaign to the Johor election context was a key factor behind its heavy loss, said analysts. International Islamic University Malaysia political scientist Assoc Prof Dr Syaza Shukri said PH had fundamentally miscalculated by campaigning on national issues. "PH's offerings during the campaign didn't excite the people.

items.title: US strikes Iran as Gulf states targeted in flareup over Hormuz
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US military on Sunday said it had launched a new wave of attacks against Iran, after renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz saw several of Washington's Gulf allies targeted by incoming fire. The latest salvo by US forces began at 2100 GMT on Sunday, Central Command (CENCTOM) said on X, after earlier announcing approximately 140 strikes the previous night. Iran reported strikes Sunday evening on two of its southern islands while Kuwait, where Teheran has repeatedly targeted US installations, said border posts and an offshore oil platform had been attacked.

items.title: BN's blue wave: Johor first, Negri Sembilan next?
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: As the dust settled following Barisan Nasional's (BN) landslide victory in the 16th Johor election, one thing became clear: BN had correctly read the mood of the electorate. Led by Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, BN's campaign focused on continuing the implementation of the development agenda, building on the groundwork laid by the state government. He said it also showed that the state administration's performance outweighed federal political considerations.

items.title: Iran: Latest US strikes have 'rendered futile' recent diplomacy
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran has condemned the latest wave of US attacks on its territory, saying they had "rendered futile" all the diplomatic efforts of the last few months. Their statement came soon after the US military announced a fresh wave of strikes against Iran on Sunday, saying it aimed to "continue degrading" Tehran's ability to attack commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The latest US salvo began at 2100 GMT on Sunday, US Central Command (CENTCOM) said on X, adding that President Donald Trump "has directed the strikes to hold Iranian forces accountable."

items.title: [UPDATED] At least 27 dead in Bangkok bar fire: PM
items.summary: BANGKOK: A massive fire in a Bangkok bar late Sunday night killed at least 27 people and injured dozens, sending screaming patrons fleeing as flames shot from the entrance, according to officials and video verified by AFP. An AFP journalist saw several black body bags on the ground outside the Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao bar and restaurant early Monday morning, while dozens of emergency service workers thronged the scene. "I heard loud screaming from a lot of people inside -- chaos happened," Laotian tourist Kan Kutirat told AFP.

items.title: Probe underway after Sg Perak turns greenish blue again
items.summary: THE Mineral and Geoscience Department and Environment Department have launched an investigation after Sungai Perak near Kampung Bukit Amarikan, Gerik, turned greenish blue again recently. Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the state government would decide on its next course of action after receiving reports of their findings. NST previously reported that Sungai Perak near Kampung Bukit Amarikan had turned greenish blue.

items.title: Lat: Don't overthink, just create
items.summary: LEGENDARY cartoonist Datuk Mohd Nor Khalid, better known as Datuk Lat, wants young artists to stop overthinking their ideas and instead focus on creating and sharing their work with the world. Lat, who received the CHT Lifetime Achievement Award for Creative Arts on Saturday, said artists should never let self-doubt or a lack of publishing opportunities stop them from showcasing their talent. Lat said while he was accustomed to working on paper, young artists today also had access to digital tools that had transformed the creative process.

items.title: Thai inflation likely below 2.8pc this year, policy to remain accommodative
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thailand's inflation is likely to come in below the Bank of Thailand's forecast of 2.8 per cent this year and ease next year, the central bank governor said on Saturday, adding that monetary policy would remain accommodative and focused on supporting the economy. Monthly inflation in the fourth quarter is now expected to be below the previously projected 4.5 per cent and ease next year, allowing the central bank to look through temporary price pressures, Governor Vitai Ratanakorn said. Headline inflation slowed to 2.42 per cent in June, remaining within the central bank's one to three per cent target range and below expectations.

items.title: Bhutan offers cash incentives to battle population crisis
items.summary: BHUTAN is offering families cash incentives to have more children as the tiny Himalayan kingdom grapples with a nosediving birthrate and a growing exodus of young people seeking opportunities abroad. Prime Minister Tshering Tobgay has repeatedly rung the alarm over the population slump, calling it an "existential" crisis, according to the state's Bhutan Broadcasting Service. "The evidence is unambiguous — Bhutan's fertility has declined to near or below replacement level," Tobgay wrote in the introduction to the government's "Third Child Plus" programme, launched last month.

items.title: Onn Hafiz announces Johor state holiday on July 13 to thank voters after election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — The Johor state government has declared tomorrow a public holiday across the state. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the special holiday was declared to thank the people of Johor for fulfilling their civic responsibilities in yesterday’s 16th state election. “I am pleased to announce that, with the approval of His Royal Highness Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, the Johor government has declared Monday, July 13, 2026 as a Johor state special holiday,” he said in a Facebook statement today.

items.title: Perodua Kancil driver killed after car crashes into reversing lorry in Setiu
items.summary: BANDAR PERMAISURI, July 12 — A senior citizen died after the Perodua Kancil car he was driving crashed into a reversing lorry at Kilometre 69, Jalan Persekutuan Kuala Terengganu-Kota Bharu, near Bandar Permaisuri, this morning. Setiu District Police Chief Supt Zahril Asri Mohd Zain said that in the incident, which occurred around 6.20am, the victim, Othman Abdullah, 62, whose address is at Kampung Guntong Luar here, passed away at the Setiu Hospital due to chest injuries. “Initial investigations revealed that the accident occurred when an UDT lorry driven by a 32-year-old local man from Segamat, Johor, exited a junction into the main road before reversing to park the vehicle.

items.title: Onn Hafiz receives appointment letter, sworn in as Johor MB after BN landslide win
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Machap Assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was today sworn in as Johor Menteri Besar before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail. The ceremony commenced with the reading of the Johor Menteri Besar's letter of appointment by the Johor State Secretary, Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir. Onn Hafiz, 48, retained the Machap state seat with a commanding majority of 15,375 votes in the 16th Johor state election, defeating Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Nur Hafiz Roslan.

items.title: Fadillah: Petronas working with PM Anwar to safeguard national energy security
items.summary: KUCHING, July 12 — Malaysia’s energy sector fuel supply remains stable, with the government closely monitoring the latest developments surrounding tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. He said Malaysia is currently not experiencing energy supply disruptions, with Petronas and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim taking proactive measures through multi-level negotiations to maintain national energy security. “Petronas has been working with the Prime Minister and engaging in negotiations at various levels to ensure our energy supply remains secure.

items.title: Desa Tun Razak PPR gets RM9.6m facelift, Hannah Yeoh says safety, comfort boosted
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Upgrading works at the Desa Tun Razak People’s Housing Project (PPR), costing RM9.6 million, have been completed, making it one of 22 out of 61 PPR upgrade projects in the capital completed so far. She said the allocation had been agreed to by all Kuala Lumpur Members of Parliament to ensure comprehensive maintenance works at PPRs, while enhancing the safety and comfort of more than 8,000 residents at the Desa Tun Razak PPR, which has been operating since 1998. Meanwhile, Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Fadhlun Mak Ujud said that of the RM9.6 million spent on the Desa Tun Razak PPR upgrading project, RM7 million was allocated for repainting works, RM1.68 million for fire prevention systems and electrical wiring upgrades, and nearly RM1 million for road resufrfacing.

items.title: Anwar extends condolences to Qatar on passing of Father Amir Sheikh Hamad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has extended Malaysia’s deepest condolences to Qatar’s Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, the Qatari royal family and the people of Qatar following the passing of the Father Amir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. In a Facebook post today, Anwar paid tribute to Sheikh Hamad for transforming Qatar into a nation of global significance during his 18-year reign as Amir. He said Qatar achieved remarkable economic progress under Sheikh Hamad’s leadership and emerged as an influential voice in regional and international affairs.

items.title: Onn Hafiz arrives at Istana Bukit Serene for Johor MB swearing‑in ceremony
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Machap assemblyman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi arrived at Istana Bukit Serene here this afternoon, believed to be to attend the swearing-in ceremony as Johor Menteri Besar. The vehicle carrying the Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman was seen entering the palace grounds at about 3.40pm, with members of the media gathered since noon to cover the ceremony. The swearing-in ceremony for the Johor Menteri Besar is expected to take place at the palace this afternoon.

items.title: Johor MB swearing‑in: Media gather at Bukit Serene as palace keeps appointee’s name secret
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — A Johor Menteri Besar is expected to be sworn in at Istana Bukit Serene here at 3pm today. According to an invitation circulated via WhatsApp, the Johor Palace announced that the swearing-in ceremony would take place at the palace this afternoon. The swearing-in of the Johor Menteri Besar is scheduled for 3pm today, July 12,” the invitation stated.

items.title: Teen escapes after Myvi plunges into Bachok river following suspected drowsy driving
items.summary: BACHOK, July 12 — A 16-year-old student escaped after the Perodua Myvi he was driving plunged into a river near the Pengkalan Petah water gate here yesterday, after he is believed to have fallen asleep while driving. Bachok Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Nor Azini Che Wil said the Form Four student from a school in Kota Bharu was travelling home after attending tuition classes when the incident occurred at about 3.10pm. Nor Azini said the vehicle had plunged into the river close to the bank, allowing the teenager to remain calm during the incident.

items.title: Johor polls disappointment for Pakatan, but Loke vows fresh push ahead of Negeri Sembilan election
items.summary: PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke said the coalition had anticipated a tough contest given the current momentum of support for the state government. Nevertheless, PH managed to secure eight out of the 56 state seats contested. “In the last Negeri Sembilan state election, PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14.

items.title: Youth vs veteran in Johor polls: Felicia Poh, 28, wins Penggaram; Samsolbari, 65, holds Semarang
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — At just 28, Felicia Poh Rui Ling became the youngest successful candidate in the 16th Johor state election, reflecting voters’ confidence in young leadership after winning the Penggaram state seat and retaining it for Pakatan Harapan (PH). The youngest DAP candidate in the election polled 24,522 votes to defeat Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Boo Chin Leong, 65, who secured 20,385 votes, giving her a majority of 4,137 votes. The Ayer Hitam Umno division chief defeated Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Muhammad Syafiq Abdul Aziz and PH candidate Ramli Abd Hamid with a commanding majority of 14,679 votes.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming stands by pledge to resign over Najib issue as Puad challenges him to quit Cabinet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming today stood by his earlier pledge to resign from the Cabinet if former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak were released without serving an appropriate sentence, as former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi challenged him to make good on his words. In a statement on his Facebook, Nga also congratulated Barisan Nasional (BN) on its landslide victory in the Johor state election, saying the coalition’s two-thirds majority came with a heavy responsibility to govern with integrity. Shortly after Nga’s statement, former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi called on the DAP vice-chairman to resign immediately instead of offering further explanations.

items.title: Policeman killed after Perodua Kelisa veers off Pasir Puteh‑Kota Bharu road, thrown from vehicle
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 12 — A policeman was killed after being thrown out of the car he was driving when the vehicle is believed to have lost control and veered off the road at Kilometre 30 of the Pasir Puteh-Kota Bharu Federal Road near Kampung Banir Belikong junction in Pasir Puteh last night. Pasir Puteh police chief Supt Saari Yaacob said in the 11.50pm incident, the 28-year-old victim, who was attached to the Setiu district police headquarters (IPD) in Terengganu, was believed to have been on his way home to Pasir Puteh from Kota Bharu. He said preliminary investigations found that the Perodua Kelisa driven by the victim lost control before veering off the road upon reaching the scene.

items.title: Selangor JPN vows strict action in Rawang bullying case, pledges full cooperation with police
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 12 — The Selangor State Education Department (JPN) is extending full cooperation in the investigation into an alleged bullying incident involving several secondary school students in Rawang, which gained public attention after going viral on social media. “The Selangor JPN takes serious note of the recent video circulating on social media regarding an incident involving student misconduct at a school in Rawang. The Selangor JPN will not compromise on any form of misconduct that could that could affect students’ safety and well-being,” the statement said.

items.title: Home Ministry to review EAIC report, vows stricter SOPs and vetting in citizenship approvals
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 12 — The Home Affairs Ministry (KDN) will review the findings and recommendations submitted by the Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission (EAIC) together with relevant agencies, including the National Registration Department (JPN), the Immigration Department (JIM) and the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), to identify areas for improvement in the management of citizenship applications through naturalisation. In a statement today, KDN said the review would focus on strengthening standard operating procedures (SOP), documentation, coordination of work processes and governance mechanisms in line with public service best practices. “KDN wishes to emphasise that the power to consider citizenship applications is subject to the provisions of the Federal Constitution and is exercised with due caution, taking into account various factors, including public interest, national security and the facts of each case.

items.title: King meets Zahid and Onn Hafiz at Istana Pasir Pelangi following BN’s sweeping Johor victory
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — The King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, granted an audience to Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr. Ahmad Zahid Hamidi at Istana Pasir Pelangi today. BN secured a landslide victory in yesterday’s state election, winning 48 of the 56 Johor state assembly seats contested. Meanwhile, Perikatan Nasional (PN), Parti Bersama Malaysia, Muda, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli), Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM), and independent candidates failed to secure any seats.

items.title: Johor’s tale of two margins: BN’s Permas towers at 29,505, Pakatan’s Simpang Jeram clings on by 170
items.summary: The biggest margin came from BN’s Permas candidate, Baharudin Mohamed Taib, who defeated PH’s Sharon Teo with 51,274 votes to her 21,769, gaining a majority of 29,505. That margin nearly quadrupled the 7,926 majority Baharudin posted in the 2022 election. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, BN’s Machap candidate, also delivered a commanding win by taking 20,382 votes over PH’s Nor Hafiz Roslan (5,007) for a 15,375-vote majority.

items.title: Terengganu says new tourism logo meets Syariah standards, turtle icon adapted for branding
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 12 — The Terengganu state government insists its new Visit Truly Terengganu 2027 logo was carefully created to comply with Islamic principles, identity and design guidelines. State Tourism Committee chairman Datuk Razali Idris said that while Terengganu now boasts several new landmarks, the turtle remains the state’s enduring icon and has been kept as the logo’s centrepiece. Classic motorcycles on display at the closing ceremony of the Terengganu Classic Festival 2.0 2026.

items.title: Singapore President Tharman’s maiden Malaysia state visit begins July 13 with ceremony, talks, trade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam will undertake a three-day state visit to Malaysia from July 13 to 15 at the invitation of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. The Foreign Ministry said the visit reciprocates His Majesty’s state visit to Singapore on May 6 and 7, 2024. This will be Tharman’s first state visit to Malaysia since assuming office as the ninth President of Singapore on Sept 14, 2023.

items.title: Former education minister Maszlee wins Puteri Wangsa, emerges lone PKR success in Johor state election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik of Pakatan Harapan (PH) won the Puteri Wangsa state seat in the 16th Johor state election yesterday. According to the official results announced by the Election Commission (EC), Maszlee secured 41,821 votes, winning the seat with a majority of 5,744 votes. In the 2022 Johor state election, the Puteri Wangsa seat was won by Muda’s Amira Aisya Abd Aziz with a majority of 7,114 votes.

items.title: Syed Saddiq to learn fate Monday as Federal Court delivers verdict on prosecution appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Muar Member of Parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman will learn his fate tomorrow when the Federal Court delivers its decision on the prosecution’s final appeal against his acquittal on four charges involving criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property and money laundering linked to funds belonging to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s youth wing, Armada. A three-member Federal Court bench comprising Court of Appeal President Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais and Justices Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali and Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah is scheduled to deliver the judgment at 9 am. The Attorney General’s Chambers filed its appeal to the Federal Court the following day.

items.title: DAP to review Johor losses after losing Jementah, Tangkak and nine other seats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — DAP Johor will conduct a comprehensive review to determine why it lost several state assembly seats in the 16th Johor election, including Jementah and Tangkak. DAP lost 11 of the 17 seats it contested, including four previously held seats in Johor Jaya, Tangkak, Jementah and Perling. BN retained its grip on Johor, sweeping 48 of 56 state seats, while PH secured just eight.

items.title: Johor polls: 55 candidates lose deposits, including all Bersama hopefuls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — A total of 55 candidates lost their deposits after failing to obtain one-eighth of the total number of votes counted for the 16th Johor State Election yesterday. Meanwhile, seven candidates from the Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition lost their deposits. In addition, all six Independent candidates, four candidates from the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA), and the sole candidates representing Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) also lost their deposits.

items.title: EC to scrutinise voters who shared marked ballot photos after Johor polls
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, July 12 — The Election Commission (EC) will conduct a detailed review into incidents involving voters who uploaded photographs of marked ballot papers on social media during the 16th Johor state election, reported Sinar Harian. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission first needed to determine whether the images were taken by ordinary voters inside polling streams or by postal voters. Ramlan said the EC would examine the matter further to ensure such incidents, particularly involving ordinary voters, do not recur in future elections.

items.title: Wan Rosdy credits BN’s Johor victory to party machinery’s commitment and fighting spirit
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 12 — Barisan Nasional’s (BN) victory in the Johor state election was the result of the commitment and fighting spirit shown by the coalition’s entire machinery, said Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail. Wan Rosdy also thanked Johor voters for fulfilling their responsibility and giving BN the mandate to continue leading the state administration. “Congratulations BN.

items.title: Two suspected lovers arrested after police chase uncovers drugs in Perlis
items.summary: ARAU, July 12 — A man and woman, suspected to be lovers, were arrested and 419.82 grams (g) of drugs worth an estimated RM13,830 was seized in Taman Sri Wang here yesterday. The police then chased the motorcycle to Taman Sri Wang, where they were arrested,” he said in a statement last night. He said an inspection of the motorcycle revealed a black bag containing 13 transparent packets of various sizes containing what was believed to be380.45 g of heroin, as well as 13 packets containing 39.7 g of methamphetamine, along with a digital scale.
